origin: hand-authored example scenario for the session-capsules MVP benchmark harness
why: Provide a deterministic, inspectable transcript-growth pattern for replay versus restore measurements without relying on hidden conversation state.

messages.content: You are a concise assistant helping benchmark replay versus session-capsule restore behavior.
Initial packet: inspect the repository, preserve provenance, and keep the benchmark design regime-stable.

step_deltas.content: I will track every measurement with explicit provenance and note any ambiguity instead of smoothing it over.
Repository map: docs/whitepaper.md, docs/benchmark-design.md, scripts/simulate_capsules.py, and scripts/llama_slot_workflow.ps1 are the starting materials.
I will treat the simulator as theory only and use the runtime benchmark for empirical evidence.
Benchmark rule: do not mix fully-in-VRAM and spill regimes in the same series, and reject runs that drift across the boundary.
I will separate controller responsibilities from llama.cpp runtime responsibilities and keep the capsule format runtime-specific for the MVP.
Slot workflow: save after each validated prompt boundary, restore into a clean slot, then measure the next full transcript request with cache reuse enabled.
I will compare replay, restore, and save costs separately before claiming a combined win.
Handoff note: write outputs so another model can continue from the run folder without needing hidden chat context.

ambiguity_notes: This is a synthetic transcript-growth scenario for systems benchmarking, not a quality benchmark for answer semantics.
The scenario assumes prompt-only evaluation is acceptable for the first MVP so slot state matches the canonical transcript exactly.
